serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup (700 g) is the standard serving size for this beverage as listed on the GrabFood menu, providing approximately 630 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition of Food — Milk-based sweetened beverage; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Whole milk, tapioca, brown sugar, heavy cream components
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board, Singapore; U.S. Department of Agriculture
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific commercial beverage. Nutrient values were adapted from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) and cross-validated against HPB Singapore data for sweetened milk beverages and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for whole milk (FDC ID 171265), tapioca pearls (FDC ID 169717), brown sugar (FDC ID 168833), and heavy cream (FDC ID 170859). The blended per-100g profile (energy 90 kcal, protein 2.0 g, fat 3.6 g, carbohydrate 12.0 g, sugar 10.0 g, sodium 30 mg) is consistent with a diluted whole-milk beverage enriched with brown sugar syrup and tapioca pearls. Values are retained with medium confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Fructose-containing beverages and milk-based sweetened drinks; tapioca analogue GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for brown sugar tapioca pearl crème brûlée milk. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on: (1) whole milk GI ~27 (University of Sydney GI Database), (2) tapioca pearls GI ~70–85 (University of Sydney GI Database, boiled tapioca), (3) brown sugar GI ~65 (sucrose-equivalent), and (4) sweet cream (negligible carbohydrate contribution). The blended beverage GI is estimated at ~65 reflecting the dominant carbohydrate contribution from brown sugar syrup and tapioca pearls diluted by the low-GI milk base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (84 g from 700 g × 12 g/100 g) ÷ 100 = 54.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (10.0 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention, per AHA 2018 guidelines.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened milk beverage; the 10.0 g sugar/100 g exceeds the AHA threshold for beverages and the 700 g large serving delivers 70 g of added sugar, which independently elevates cardiovascular and blood pressure risk regardless of the relatively low sodium content (30 mg/100 g).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 10.0 g sugar/100 g, high-GI tapioca pearls, and negligible dietary fibre (0.2 g/100 g) causing rapid postprandial glucose excursions.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage entirely; the combination of 70 g free sugar and high-GI tapioca pearls in a 700 g serving produces a glycemic load of approximately 54.6, causing rapid and sustained blood glucose spikes that are incompatible with glycaemic management per ADA 2024 Standards of Care.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (2.0 g/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and high free sugar (10.0 g/100 g) in a large-volume beverage contribute to atherogenic lipid profiles and endothelial dysfunction.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this beverage; a 700 g serving provides 14 g saturated fat, 0.7 g trans fat, and 70 g free sugar, all of which are independently associated with elevated LDL cholesterol, triglycerides, and cardiovascular event risk per ACC/AHA 2019 guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar (10.0 g/100 g) and saturated fat from sweet cream promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage; the high free sugar and saturated fat content promote systemic inflammation and are inconsistent with anti-inflammatory dietary patterns recommended for RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Brown Sugar Deerioca Crème Brûlée Milk (Large) is a popular Singaporean-Chinese chilled milk beverage sold in large cup format (700 g). It is assembled by combining fresh whole milk with brown sugar syrup, chewy Deerioca tapioca pearls, and a crème brûlée-flavoured pudding layer, then topped with a dollop of sweet cream. The drink mimics the caramelised, custard-like flavour profile of classic French crème brûlée in a cold beverage format. The texture is creamy and rich from the milk and cream, with the chewy, slightly sticky tapioca pearls providing contrast. The brown sugar syrup imparts a deep molasses-like sweetness. Per 100 g, the beverage provides approximately 90 kcal, 2.0 g protein, 3.6 g fat (2.0 g saturated), 12.0 g carbohydrate (10.0 g sugar), 0.2 g dietary fibre, and 30 mg sodium. A standard large serving (700 g) delivers approximately 630 kcal and 70 g of free sugar, making it a high-calorie, high-sugar beverage. Clinically, this drink is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high glycaemic load (~54.6 per serving), high free sugar content, and saturated fat from cream. It is vegetarian and likely halal. The beverage is part of the broader brown sugar bubble tea trend that originated in Taiwan and became widely popular across Singapore and Southeast Asia from 2018 onwards.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific commercial beverage. Nutrient values are adapted from the ai_generated input (confidence 0.7) and cross-validated against HPB Singapore sweetened milk beverage data and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries. The per-100g profile is internally consistent and plausible for the described formulation. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific commercial brown sugar tapioca pearl milk beverage are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16; no approved source reports these values for this formulation. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): fresh milk and brown sugar syrup contain negligible vitamin C; this is consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for whole milk (FDC ID 171265) which reports 0 mg vitamin C. trans_fat_g is 0.1 g/100 g as reported in the input record, consistent with trace trans fats naturally present in dairy fat. The two portions in the input record both carry is_default: true; only the large cup (700 g) is used as the canonical serving size for this large-format product. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this specific product is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database.
